title: pyrus bumthangensis
intro: Pyrus bumthangensis, also known as Bhutan cherry or Himalayan cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating cough and sore throat, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and fruit of Pyrus bumthangensis are used, often in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, to harness its therapeutic properties.
